closure_statement: Registry interaction is represented through Dispatcher, Registry / Delivery Port, readiness/admission contracts, and explicit lifecycle transfer boundaries rather than through registry-internal ownership.
risk_prevented: Blind handoff, registry-internal coupling, Dispatcher replacement by port, and assembler ownership of registry-side lifecycle.
delegated_entity_names: Assembler Subsystem;Dispatcher;Registry / Delivery Port;Log Level Records Registry;Reading / Querying Subsystem
governed_object_names: Readiness Signal;Receiving Admission Access;Lifecycle Transfer State;Dispatcher Boundary Edge;Non-Authority Boundary;Log Level Envelope;Delivery-Cycle Flag Set;Dispatcher Feedback Report
anti_regression_rule: Registry / Delivery Port may expose the contract surface, but Dispatcher remains the outbound actor and assembler lifecycle authority ends after valid handoff.
notes: G-001 is covered across readiness, delivery, boundary exclusion, and lifecycle transfer traces.

closure_statement: Policy source, policy bundle intake, Policy Object identity, assignment mediation, assignment surfaces, and component-local mechanism hosting are separated into explicit responsibilities and obligations.
risk_prevented: Policy treated as anonymous config, component self-sourcing policies, Policy Object becoming executor, and administrative assignment collapsing into managerial execution.
delegated_entity_names: PolicyAssigner;Administrative / Ecosystem Port;Dispatcher;Writing / Feeding Round Manager;Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager;Queue Bundle Agent;Single Queue Moderator;Timestamp Stabilizer;Validator;Metadata Injector;Assemblers Administrator;Policy Registry / Policy Source;Metadata Directive Source
governed_object_names: AssemblerPolicyBundle;Policy Object;Metadata Directive;Administrative Surface;Mechanism Semantics;Metadata Schema;Metadata Fields
anti_regression_rule: PolicyAssigner assigns; Policy Object governs semantics; host components realize mechanisms locally; Metadata Injector remains schema/directive-governed and is not a policy host.
notes: G-002 is covered by policy source granularity and by explicit exclusion of Metadata Injector from policy hosting.

closure_statement: Dispatcher delivery-cycle state flags and dispatcher detailed feedback reports are represented as two separate governed artifacts with separate sub-responsibility groups.
risk_prevented: Conflating compact delivery-cycle state with detailed diagnostic/reporting artifacts; losing delivery outcome semantics; mixing flags with reports.
delegated_entity_names: Dispatcher;Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager;Single Queue Moderator;Registry / Delivery Port;Log Level Records Registry;Optional diagnostics/reporting consumer
governed_object_names: Delivery-Cycle Flag Set;Dispatcher Feedback Report;Readiness Signal;Receiving Admission Access;Dispatcher Boundary Edge;Slot Lifecycle State;Queue Container Identifier;Log Level Envelope
anti_regression_rule: BCO-013 Delivery-Cycle Flag Set is compact state; BCO-014 Dispatcher Feedback Report is detailed diagnostic/reporting artifact; no row may treat them as the same object.
notes: G-003 is explicitly expanded into seven flag sub-responsibilities and five report sub-responsibilities.

closure_statement: Timestamp Stabilizer is no longer a placeholder; it is a mature, mandatory preparation component participating across accepted ingress and envelope assembly flows.
risk_prevented: Timestamp slot remaining undefined, timestamp semantics confused with envelope timing slot placeholder, and optional timestamp stabilization.
governed_object_names: Timestamp Value;Envelope Timestamp Field;Log Level Envelope;Metadata Fields;Ingress Contract Variants
anti_regression_rule: Timestamp Stabilizer is a mature mandatory component; no placeholder timing-slot semantics may remain where stabilized timestamp is required.
notes: G-004 is closed by tracing timestamp stabilization to Timestamp Stabilizer and envelope composition.

closure_statement: Queue Bundle Agent, Single Queue Moderator, Writing / Feeding Round Manager, and Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager are separated by bundle-level, single queue-container, writing-round, and delivery-round responsibilities.
risk_prevented: Collapsing bundle orchestration into single queue moderation, using Queue Runtime Layer as delegated actor, losing round-specific waiting-list discipline, or obscuring operational directive effects.
delegated_entity_names: Writing / Feeding Round Manager;Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager;Queue Bundle Agent;Single Queue Moderator;Administrative / Ecosystem Port;PolicyAssigner
governed_object_names: Operational Directive;Queue Bundle;Queue Container;Slot;Slot Lifecycle State;Writing Round Waiting List;Delivery Round Waiting List;Queue Container Identifier;Policy Object;Delivery-Cycle Flag Set;Dispatcher Feedback Report
anti_regression_rule: Queue Bundle Agent orchestrates bundles; Single Queue Moderator moderates one queue-container; round managers own round waiting discipline; Queue Runtime Layer remains classification-only.
notes: G-005 is covered by explicit role separation across queue runtime actors and round managers.

closure_statement: Queue equals Container; slots are contained inside queue-container semantics; occupancy is binding/state; waiting lists store Queue Container Identifiers; envelope lifecycle remains separate and transfers only at valid dispatcher-registry handoff.
risk_prevented: Introducing a separate runtime container, treating waiting lists as envelope stores, making occupancy lifecycle owner, confusing Queue Container Identifier with envelope identity, or conflating queue-container ownership with Log Level Envelope lifecycle.
delegated_entity_names: Dispatcher;Writing / Feeding Round Manager;Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager;Queue Bundle Agent;Single Queue Moderator;Assembler Ingress Port;Registry / Delivery Port
governed_object_names: Queue Bundle;Queue Container;Slot;Envelope Slot Occupancy;Slot Lifecycle State;Writing Round Waiting List;Delivery Round Waiting List;Queue Container Identifier;Log Level Envelope;Lifecycle Transfer State;Dispatcher Boundary Edge;Delivery-Cycle Flag Set;Dispatcher Feedback Report
anti_regression_rule: No separate runtime container may be introduced; waiting lists store Queue Container Identifiers; Envelope Slot Occupancy is binding/state only; Log Level Envelope lifecycle transfers only at valid handoff.
notes: G-006 is the strongest runtime terminology closure and anchors the corrected queue-container model.

closure_statement: Validating ingress, hot-path ingress, typed/template contracts, Envelope Assembler, Validator, Timestamp Stabilizer, and Assembler Ingress Port are separated into explicit contract and responsibility traces.
risk_prevented: Collapsing validating and hot-path paths, treating contract layer as runtime COM, bypassing Envelope Assembler, or hiding validation authority placement.
delegated_entity_names: Metadata Injector;Timestamp Stabilizer;Validator;Envelope Assembler;Assembler Ingress Port;Administrative / Ecosystem Port;Level API;Metadata Directive Source
governed_object_names: Typed Content Contract;Ingress Contract Variants;Administrative Surface;Validation Candidate Input;Metadata Directive;Log Level Envelope;Metadata Fields;Metadata Schema;Timestamp Value;Envelope Timestamp Field
anti_regression_rule: Envelope Assembler is the client-side composed contract object; Validator participates only in validating ingress; hot-path must not silently reintroduce validation; contracts remain typed/template laws.
notes: G-007 is covered through ingress contract variants, Envelope Assembler composition, and explicit Validator scope.
